free_text: Nuclear reactions of Au-197 with 11.5 and 300-GeV
protons.
Zero gradient synchrotron at 11.5-GeV
The Fermi National Accelerator
laboratory at 300-GeV.
At 11.5-GeV.
At 300-GeV.
Targets consisted of three aluminum foils (each of
7-mg/cm**2 thickness) upstream of three gold target
foils (each of 24-mg/cm**2 thickness). After the
irradiation the stack was cut 1-cm back from the
leading edge and the center aluminum and gold foils
were weighed to determine their exact thickness prior
to counting.
At 300-GeV irradiation, similar target stacks to those
described above were enclosed in evacuated polyethylene
bags, and after the irradiation a portion of the stack
centered on the beam spot was cut out.
Total uncertainty includes:
uncertainty in the absolute efficiency
calibrations of the detectors
uncertainty in the secondary correction for
Na-24 in the monitors.
Uncertainties in the gamma-ray abundances were not
included separately since agreement between the
observed relative intensities and the literature values
was one of the requirements for the acceptability of
the gamma-ray for cross section determinations.
Tbl.II from Phys.Rev.,C14(1976)1121
Subentry O0690045 has been corrected
according to O.Schwerer's remarks.
Last checking has been done.
